data.title: n8n Review: Why I Switched My Entire Workflow
data.excerpt: n8n lets you build complex automations with 400+ integrations and AI nodes. Here's why I chose it over every other tool I tested.

$media.alt: n8n Review: Why I Switched My Entire Workflow

children.text: *Disclosure: This post contains affiliate links. If you sign up through them, I may earn a small commission at no extra cost to you.*
I've tried more automation tools than I care to admit. Some were too limited. Others charged me per task until my bill looked like a phone number. Then I found
n8n
, and it changed how I think about building workflows.

This isn't another "everything is great" review. I'm going to walk you through exactly why n8n earned a permanent spot in my stack — from its open-source roots to its AI-powered workflow builder that genuinely surprised me.
Why I Keep Coming Back to n8n
The first thing that hooked me was the
self-hosting option
. As someone who cares about data ownership, knowing I could run n8n on my own server felt like a relief. No more wondering where my API keys and customer data live.

But here's the thing — even if you don't want to self-host, n8n's cloud version works flawlessly. I started with the cloud, loved it, and eventually moved some workflows to self-hosted for specific client projects. The flexibility is unmatched.
What really sets n8n apart is the
visual editor

. I drag nodes, connect them, and watch complex logic come together without writing a single line of code. When I *do* need custom logic, I drop in a JavaScript or Python node right inside the workflow. No switching tools. No exporting data. Everything stays contained.
If you've been curious about
what n8n actually is and how it works
, I wrote a full breakdown that covers the fundamentals.
What Makes n8n Stand Out: Features I Actually Use
Let me be specific. These aren't features listed on a landing page that I skimmed. These are the ones I use *weekly*:
AI Workflow Builder

This is n8n's newest powerhouse. You describe what you want in plain English, and the AI suggests a workflow for you. I typed "Summarize incoming Slack messages and send a daily digest to my email" — and n8n built the skeleton in seconds.
It's not perfect on the first try, but it gets you 80% there. The remaining 20% is just tweaking a few nodes. Compare that to building from scratch, and you're saving hours every month.
400+ Integrations (and Growing)

Google Sheets, Airtable, Notion, HubSpot, PostgreSQL, Telegram, WhatsApp, Stripe — they're all there. I've never hit a wall where n8n didn't have the integration I needed. And if a specific API isn't available as a native node, the
HTTP Request node
handles any REST API you throw at it.
For those exploring chat-based automations, I've paired n8n with tools like
Wati.io
for WhatsApp campaigns and the results have been impressive.
Sub-Workflows

This one's underrated. You can call one workflow from another, passing data between them. I use this for modular design — my main workflow stays clean, and complex logic lives in reusable sub-workflows. If something changes, I update it once and every parent workflow benefits.
Error Workflows

When something fails, n8n doesn't just stop. I've set up error workflows that notify me on Slack, log the error to a spreadsheet, and even retry the failed node automatically. This alone has saved me from multiple "oh no" moments at 2 AM.
Concurrency and Execution Controls
On the Pro plan, you get
20 concurrent executions
. That means 20 workflows running at the same time without queuing. For time-sensitive automations — like processing incoming orders or responding to form submissions — this speed matters.
Want to dive deeper into practical applications? Check out my guide on
n8n use cases
that goes beyond the obvious.
Who n8n Is Ideal For
I've recommended n8n to different people, and here's who benefits the most:
Solo founders and indie hackers

— You're wearing every hat. n8n handles the repetitive work so you can focus on building product. At $20/month for the Starter plan, it's cheaper than most alternatives and doesn't cap your steps per workflow.
Small marketing teams

— Automate lead routing, email sequences, CRM updates, and reporting dashboards. I've seen teams cut their manual data entry by 90% after a single afternoon of setup. If you're already using tools like
Bürch (Revealbot)
for ad management, n8n connects those dots beautifully.
Developers who want low-code without the limitations

— n8n doesn't insult your intelligence. You can write code when you need to. The JavaScript and Python nodes are full-featured, not watered-down sandboxes. It's the sweet spot between "no-code" and "just write a script."
Agencies managing multiple clients

— The Business plan supports shared projects and admin roles. You can organize workflows by client, set permissions, and collaborate without stepping on each other's toes. With 40K executions per month, you've got room to scale.
Anyone tired of per-task pricing

— n8n counts *workflow executions*, not individual steps within a workflow. One execution might include 50 nodes, and it still counts as one. That's fundamentally different from tools that charge per action.
Here's where I'd start if you're serious about trying it —
explore n8n's plans and grab a free trial
. No credit card required.
Real Workflows I've Built (and How They Saved My Time)
Theory is nice. Results are better. Here are three workflows I'm running right now:
1. Content Pipeline Automation
When I publish a new blog post, n8n automatically:
Adds it to my Notion content database
Generates social media captions using OpenAI
Schedules posts via Buffer
Sends a Slack notification to my team
Time saved:
~45 minutes per post.
2. Lead Qualification Flow
New leads from a Typeform submission get:
Scored based on company size and budget answers
Added to HubSpot with the correct lifecycle stage
Routed to the right sales rep on Slack
Sent a personalized email via Gmail
Time saved:
~2 hours per day of manual triage.
3. Weekly Reporting Dashboard
Every Monday at 9 AM, n8n:
Pulls data from Google Analytics, Stripe, and Mailchimp
Calculates key metrics (MRR, churn, open rates)
Formats everything into a Google Sheet
Posts a summary to a private Telegram channel
Time saved:
~3 hours per week of spreadsheet wrangling.
If you want step-by-step instructions, I wrote a complete
tutorial on how to use n8n
that walks through building your first automation from zero.
n8n Pricing: Why It's a No-Brainer
Let's talk numbers, because this is where n8n really shines compared to the competition.
Starter Plan — $20/month
2,500 workflow executions
Unlimited steps per execution
Unlimited users
50 AI Workflow Builder credits
Hosted by n8n (zero server setup)
Pro Plan — $50/month
10,000 workflow executions
20 concurrent executions
7 days of insights
150 AI Workflow Builder credits
Admin roles, global variables, workflow history
Business Plan — €667/month
40,000 workflow executions
Self-hosted option
Advanced collaboration features
Priority support
Every plan includes a
free trial with no credit card required
. You can build and test real workflows before committing a single dollar.
The annual billing saves you
, which is meaningful when you're running a lean operation. And remember — one execution can contain unlimited steps. You're not being nickel-and-dimed for every API call inside a workflow.
Ready to see what 10,000 executions a month actually feels like?
Start your free n8n trial today
Getting Started: What I'd Do Differently
If I could go back and give myself advice on day one, here's what I'd say:
Start with a template.
n8n has a massive library of pre-built workflows. Don't build from scratch when someone's already solved your problem. Browse the
template library
, clone what looks close, and customize from there.
Use sticky notes in the canvas.
It sounds trivial, but when a workflow has 20+ nodes, sticky notes explaining each section save you during debugging.
Test with the Manual Trigger first.

Before you set a workflow to run on a schedule or webhook, test every step manually. n8n lets you execute individual nodes and inspect the data flowing between them. This catches 90% of issues before they become real problems.
Learn expressions early.

n8n's expression language lets you transform data inline — format dates, extract fields, run conditional logic. Once you're comfortable with `{{ $json.fieldName }}`, your workflows become dramatically more powerful.
Don't fear the Code node.

Even if you're "not a developer," the Code node is approachable. Write a few lines of JavaScript to handle edge cases that visual nodes can't cover. n8n's documentation and community are incredibly helpful here.
And speaking of the community — the
latest n8n features
are often driven by user feedback. The team ships fast and listens.
The Bottom Line

n8n isn't just another automation tool. It's an open-source platform that respects your data, doesn't punish you for building complex workflows, and keeps adding features that actually matter — like AI-assisted workflow building and sub-workflow modularity.

Whether you're a solo operator automating your first process or a team running hundreds of daily executions, n8n scales with you. The pricing is honest. The product is powerful. The community is active.
I've automated over 200 workflows with n8n, and I'm still discovering new ways to use it. That's the mark of a tool built with depth, not just surface-level polish.
Give n8n a try — free trial, no credit card

Frequently Asked Questions
Is n8n really free?
Yes. n8n is open-source, so you can self-host it completely free on your own server. If you prefer managed hosting, the cloud plans start at $20/month with a free trial and no credit card required.
How is n8n different from Zapier or Make?

The biggest difference is pricing structure and flexibility. n8n counts workflow executions, not individual actions — one workflow with 50 steps counts as a single execution. It's also open-source, meaning you can self-host and own your data. The Code node lets you write custom JavaScript or Python directly inside workflows, which neither Zapier nor Make offers at the same level.
Can I use n8n if I'm not a developer?

nAbsolutely. The visual drag-and-drop editor is designed for non-technical users. The AI Workflow Builder generates entire automations from plain English descriptions. You can start with templates and customize without writing code. That said, developers will appreciate the Code nodes and API flexibility when they need it.
Does n8n support AI and machine learning workflows?

Yes. n8n includes native AI nodes for OpenAI, Hugging Face, and other providers. You can chain AI calls with other integrations — for example, summarizing text with GPT, storing results in Airtable, and notifying a Slack channel, all in one workflow.
What happens if my workflow fails?

You can configure dedicated error workflows in n8n. When a workflow fails, your error handler can send notifications, log the error, retry the failed step, or take any corrective action you define. The Pro plan also includes execution search and 7 days of workflow history for debugging.

children.text: Is n8n really free to use?
Yes. n8n is open-source and can be self-hosted at no cost. Cloud-hosted plans start at $20/month with a free trial that requires no credit card.
How does n8n differ from Zapier or Make?

n8n counts full workflow executions, not individual actions, making complex automations far more cost-effective. It's also open-source with self-hosting options, and includes Code nodes for custom JavaScript or Python.
Can non-developers use n8n effectively?
Absolutely. n8n's visual editor and AI Workflow Builder let anyone create automations from plain English descriptions. Templates provide starting points, and no coding is required for most use cases.
Does n8n integrate with AI tools like OpenAI?
Yes. n8n has native nodes for OpenAI, Hugging Face, and other AI providers. You can build workflows that chain AI calls with 400+ other integrations seamlessly.
